L1.area: Data structure operations and memory management logic

L2.description: A buffer overflow vulnerability exists where an element insertion operation on a collection data structure does not validate the upper bound of the provided index, allowing an out-of-bounds write into memory beyond the allocated buffer.

L3.area: Vector trait default method implementations for element insertion and manipulation
L3.description: A buffer overflow vulnerability exists where an element insertion operation on a fixed-capacity array-backed vector does not validate the upper bound of the provided index before performing unsafe pointer arithmetic and memory copy operations, allowing an out-of-bounds write.

ground_truth.reason: The Vector::insert method does not check if the provided index exceeds the current length, allowing unsafe pointer arithmetic to write beyond the allocated buffer.
